5.0:
Yo, Austin Kleon said it best—
Nothing's fully original, we just remix the lineage, honest.
Steal like an artist... let's flip it for today.
[Chorus]
Nothing's fully original, that's the truth we inherit,
Remix your lineage, make it honest, no counterfeit.
From the ancestors' bars to the pixels on my screen,
Every idea's a seed—water it, let it breathe.
Steal like an artist, transform what you admire,
Mash it with your fire, take it higher, never tire.
In the age of AI and infinite scrolls,
Remix your bloodline, claim your soul!
[Verse 1]
Back in the day, Picasso peeped the Africans, flipped the forms,
Shakespeare borrowed plots, made 'em his own norms.
T.S. Eliot said immature poets imitate,
Mature ones steal—then elevate the state.
Kleon dropped the book: "Nothing comes from nowhere,"
Every track, every app, every fresh idea's a remix affair.
You a product of the books you read, the beats you bang,
The family stories, the late-night YouTube gang.
My Vietnamese roots in the phở broth wisdom,
Mixed with Bay Area hustle—Silicon vision.
Track timing apps from old stopwatches and Supabase dreams,
PE lessons turned code—remixing the team.
Don't front like you invented the wheel,
Honor the lineage, that's the real deal.
Copy your heroes till you find your twist,
Then break the mold— that's how legends persist.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: Scroll the feed, but curate the feast,
AI spits drafts? Good—now remix 'em with your beast.
Kid drawing prompts from old masters + Pokémon flair,
Family tree app with Harada grids in the layers.
Music industry? Samples been the game since '88,
Kanye flipped soul, now Grok helps generate.
TikTok dances? Straight lineage from breakbeats and vogue,
Memes evolve daily—remix or get rogue.
Day trading charts? Patterns from generations past,
But your edge is the psychology, emotions you mash.
Don't plagiarize—transform, credit the source,
Build in public, show the lineage, stay on course.
In schools, apps, and startups, same rule applies:
Take the best of what's been, synthesize.
No more paralysis from "gotta be 100% new,"
Embrace the mashup—watch your vision breakthrough.
[Bridge] (Slower, reflective—maybe with vocal chops)
Honest thieves don't hide the scars...
We wear 'em proud, connect the stars...
From grandma's tales to coder bars...
Remix your lineage, that's who we are.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So to the creators in Milpitas grinding late,
Teachers building tools, parents flipping fate.
Son's vocal tic? Turn pain into rhythm and grace,
Wife's support in the background—lineage embrace.
Steal from Kleon, from Ohtani's grind, from every app that came before,
Then drop something fresh that opens new doors.
Nothing's fully original—own that fact,
Remix honest, stay intact.
Lineage loud, future stacked...
Yeah, steal like an artist—now go make it crack.
